title: All 30 Dow stocks are falling, with Goldman Sachs and Salesforce the biggest drags
text: The Dow Jones Industrial Average's DJIA, -1.60% is suffering a unanimous decline of 480 points, or 1.6%, as all 30 of the blue-chip barometer's components are trading lower. The biggest point drags are the shares of Salesforce Inc. CRM, -4.96% , which are losing $8.05, or 4.7%, and of Goldman Sachs Group Inc. GS, -2.90% , which are down $6.61, or 2.2%; the combined price declines are shaving about 97 points off the Dow's price.

title: Goldman Sachs partners with Derivative Path to grow transaction banking
text: Goldman Sachs Group Inc said on Thursday it is launching a partnership with capital markets technology firm Derivative Path that opens the door to thousands of U.S. regional banks and credit unions becoming clients of its foreign exchange services.

title: Buy Goldman Sachs, Analysts Say. Its Stock Offers Shelter From the Economic Storm.
text: Goldman Sachs stock looks cheap and should hold up better in an economic downturn than the banking sector overall, Bank of America analysts say.

title: Goldman Sachs: Stress Test Results Could Set the Stock Free
text: Goldman Sachs ( GS , Financial) is one of the 34 big banks that passed its stress test earlier this month, in turn causing investor optimism. The market's been unkind to Goldman stock during the past few months as its cyclical nature hasn't taken well to the bear market, but now, it appears there's cause for optimism.

title: Bank of America upgrades Goldman Sachs on relative performance to other banks, but cuts earnings view
text: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. GS, +1.54% rose 1.6% after Bank of America analyst Ebrahim H. Poonawala upgraded the company to buy from neutral on his view that the bank offers a relatively better value than its peers in a looming economic downturn.

title: Analyst: big banks have an edge over their smaller rivals
text: The Invesco KBW Bank ETF is down 30% from its year-to-date high; still, a Senior Research Analyst at Piper Sandler says bank stocks are a “decent” investment amidst the market uncertainty. Jeffery Harte prefers the mega-cap bank stocks U.S. Federal Reserve has already confirmed that the big banks are well-positioned to weather a looming recession.

title: Goldman, Wells Fargo lead dividend buffet after Fed stress test
text: Six of the U.S.'s largest banks said they have enough capital to either maintain or hike their dividends to shareholders after setting enough aside to handle the most extreme economic conditions expected in the coming year.

title: carsales.com Ltd to purchase 100% of Trader Interactive
text: The largest online marketplace in Australia to add Trader Interactive's US marketplaces to their brand portfolio     VIRGINIA BEACH, Va. , June 27, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- carsales.com Ltd (carsales), the largest online marketplace in Australia focusing on the automotive, motorcycle, and marine realms, has announced the intention to purchase the remaining 51% of Trader Interactive from the current investors, Eurazeo and Goldman Sachs Asset Management to bring their holding up to 100%.

title: U.S. Fed says banks can weather severe downturn comfortably
text: The largest U.S. banks would remain well capitalized in the event of a severe economic shock, the U.S. Federal Reserve said on Thursday after the lenders' annual health check, paving the way for them to issue buybacks and dividends.

title: Bank stocks jump, then quickly pull back after Fed rate hike
text: Financial stocks spiked up, then pulled back after the Federal Reserve raised its target on overnight rates by 0.75 percentage points on Wednesday. Although the 75-basis-point hike was expected, it was still historic, as it was the biggest increase in nearly three decades, and was the Fed's way of saying it will act aggressively to fight inflation.

title: AlphaSense's Valuation Jumps to $1.7 Billion with $225 Million Series D Led by Goldman Sachs Asset Management and Viking Global
text: Increase in valuation driven by strong business performance at significant scale, with AlphaSense more than doubling its user and customer base in the past year and surpassing $100 million in recurring revenue NEW YORK , June 15, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- New York-based AlphaSense, the leading market intelligence and search platform, today announced its $225 million Series D financing round led by the Growth Equity business within Goldman Sachs Asset Management (Goldman Sachs) and Viking Global Investors. The financing values the company at $1.7 billion, which is nearly double the valuation from its  $180 million Series C funding in September 2021 that was also led by Goldman Sachs and Viking Global.

title: RippleMatch Raises $45m from Goldman Sachs Asset Management to Become the Way Gen Z Finds Work
text: NEW YORK , June 8, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- RippleMatch, the recruitment automation platform changing how Gen Z finds work, announced a $45m Series B today led by the Growth Equity business within Goldman Sachs Asset Management (Goldman Sachs). The heavily oversubscribed round followed a year of rapid growth in which RippleMatch grew its user base 3x and drove hundreds of thousands of interviews for customers including SAP, General Mills, and Amazon.
